vulnerability.reference: LLM judges sharing architectures and training paradigms produce correlated blind spots, overestimating evaluation consensus.
vulnerability.generated: Researchers’ trust in inter-model agreement as a signal of correctness, without verifying error independence across LLM judges.

impact.reference: Flawed models mistakenly deemed safe, risking deployment of harmful or biased systems.
impact.generated: Hidden systematic failures inflate agreement scores, producing overconfident and misleading model evaluations that misguide research and deployment choices.

dose_response.reference: Correlated errors cause systematic failure alignment, which when researchers use LLM judges inflates inter-evaluator agreement, leading to deployment of flawed models as safe.
dose_response.generated: Correlated errors in LLM predictions, through researchers using LLM judges to evaluate outputs, triggers trust in inter-model agreement as a correctness signal unchecked for error independence, leading to hidden systematic failures that inflate agreement scores and produce overconfident, misleading evaluations.
